summary: Raw early set with Hard To Handle energy, but audio drops later
blurb: The first half bursts with high‑energy renditions of Hard To Handle, The Other One and a rousing Casey Jones, highlighted by Jerry’s ripping guitar and Tom Constanten’s early keyboards. Audio issues after Doin’ That Rag cause drop‑outs that dull the momentum, making later numbers like Let Me In less effective.
review:
July 4 1969 at Chicago’s Electric Theater captures the Grateful Dead in a raw, high‑octane state. The set kicks off with a ferocious Hard To Handle and rolls through country‑flavored tunes before diving into psychedelic explorations on The Other One and High Time, all propelled by Jerry Garcia’s ripping guitar work and even a surprising pedal‑steel flavor on early numbers. Tom Constanten’s organ adds color to the standout Casey Jones, while Bob Weir leads vocal duties on Dire Wolf and other classics. Unfortunately, after Doin’ That Rag the tape begins to betray the band – left‑channel hiss, pulsing drop‑outs and occasional cuts obscure the performance, especially on Let Me In, leaving the momentum stalled for the remainder of the show. Despite these technical setbacks, gems like Turn On Your Lovelight and a spirited Dire Wolf keep the set memorable, making this recording a valuable snapshot of the Dead’s early evolution.

Both available soundboard sources suffer from tape hiss and intermittent drop‑outs, but the Sirmick remaster (gd69-07-04.sbd.sirmick.remaster.29294.shnf) offers the clearest capture of the first half’s brilliance, preserving the energy that makes this early show worth a listen for completists and fans of the band’s formative era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings describe a mixed sentiment – strong praise for early songs (Hard To Handle, The Other One, Casey Jones) but consistent criticism of tape hiss and drop‑outs that mar later portions. No single theme of overwhelming positivity or negativity emerges, so the adjustment is neutral.

best_recording.reason: Provides the clearest early‑set sound and captures the standout performances despite later tape issues.

key_highlights: Early set electrified by Hard To Handle and The Other One with ripping guitars.
Jerry’s pedal‑steel accents and Tom Constanten’s organ on Casey Jones add psychedelic flavor.
Doin’ That Rag marks the turning point where tape drop‑outs begin, dulling momentum.
Despite audio woes, standout moments include Dire Wolf and Turn On Your Lovelight.

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping guitar work on Hard To Handle and The Other One; pedal‑steel accents praised on early numbers; vocal tone highlighted on Casey Jones.
band_performance.Bob: Dominates vocals, leading most songs including Dire Wolf and Casey Jones.
band_performance.Tom: Keyboard work stands out on the early Casey Jones rendition, adding organ texture to the set.
